title: Agentic Pattern Code Examples
description: One shard per category under examples-src/<category>.json. Each entry attaches a list of code examples to a pattern_id that resolves in patterns-src/. The examples themselves embed the code as a string, so this JSON is the single source of truth — there are no separate .py / .ts files on disk. The HTML site renders each pattern's examples as a tabbed code panel (one tab per example); the rendered markdown page shows them as sequential fenced code blocks.

category.description: Must match the shard's category in patterns-src/.

label.description: Human-readable category label (mirrors the patterns-src shard).

pattern_id.description: Must resolve to a pattern id in patterns-src/<this-category>.json.

intent.description: One sentence on what these examples collectively demonstrate. Optional; often copies the pattern's intent slot.

framework.description: Framework id. Either 'pseudo' (language-agnostic skeleton), or a framework id that resolves to a kind=framework composition in compositions-src/.

framework_label.description: Human-readable framework name used as the tab label. Falls back to a title-cased version of `framework` when absent.

language.description: Primary language of the code. Drives syntax-highlight class in the rendered output.

code.description: The example source code, embedded as a string. Newlines are real \n line breaks. Keep compact — one pattern per example, no composition.

intent.description: One-sentence description of what this specific example demonstrates.

source_url.description: URL of the canonical upstream documentation or example this code mirrors. Used during verification.

sdk_version.description: Best-known package version constraint at write time (e.g. 'langchain>=1.0.0' or '@anthropic-ai/sdk@0.40.x').

verified.description: True once a human or CI has run/inspected this example against source_url at sdk_version. New examples ship with verified=false.

verified_date.description: ISO date the example was last verified. Null when verified=false.

verified_notes.description: Free-text notes from verification — what was run, what was checked, what would break it.

last_updated.description: ISO date this example was last modified.
